headline: Fed’s Schmid sees inflation close to 3% amid energy price surge
summary: Investing.com -- Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City President Jeff Schmid said Tuesday that the central bank should not overlook the inflation impact from rising energy prices linked to the Iran conflict.

headline: Analyst cuts Colgate-Palmolive on rising input costs, slower earnings growth
summary: Investing.com -- TD Cowen downgraded Colgate-Palmolive to Hold from Buy on Tuesday, warning that rising costs tied to oil-based inputs and tallow may pressure earnings over the next several quarters.

headline: High Oil Prices Will Hurt Colgate-Palmolive Stock According to Wall Street
summary: Colgate-Palmolive (NYSE:CL) lost a key Wall Street backer on Tuesday as TD Cowen analyst Robert Moskow downgraded the stock from Buy to Hold and cut his price target to $85 from $96, citing surging oil-based input costs tied to the Iran War. The move signals that even a consumer staples stalwart with 63 consecutive years ... High Oil Prices Will Hurt Colgate-Palmolive Stock According to Wall Street

headline: Canada reports 0.1% GDP growth in January despite auto sector decline
summary: Investing.com -- The Canadian economy maintained a modest growth trajectory at the start of the year, supported by a resurgence in the extraction and construction sectors. Real gross domestic product nudged up 0.1% in January, following a revised 0.2% expansion in the final month of 2025. Goods-producing industries led the advancement with a 0.2% increase, primarily fueled by a rebound in oil and gas extraction. headline: Colgate Palmolive (CL) Valuation Check After Deutsche Bank Upgrade And Growth Narrative Reassessment
summary: Colgate-Palmolive (CL) is back in focus after Deutsche Bank upgraded the stock to Buy, citing the company’s franchise quality, long-term 2030 Strategy investments, and resilience during periods of market volatility. See our latest analysis for Colgate-Palmolive. Colgate-Palmolive’s 1-day share price return of 1.71% and 7.82% 90-day share price return sit against a 13.53% 30-day pullback. The 1-year total shareholder return of a 6.25% decline contrasts with 3-year and 5-year gains above 20%...
